response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong software engineer with broad full-stack and distributed-systems experience, but the role demands deep C++ expertise as its core requirement, and his C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no evident background in industrial automation, IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle, MQTT, or the industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850) the role targets, and no Azure experience is mentioned. The experience breadth is impressive, but the technology alignment with this specific role is too weak to justify moving forward.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience with consistent seniority progression and hands-on architectural responsibility across distributed, event-driven systems

Demonstrated ability with REST APIs, message-broker patterns, and high-throughput micro-services (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second), which partially maps to the role's networking and scalability requirements
Strong agile and software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring via workshops) aligns with the behavioral competencies and team-mentorship expectations
concerns: C++ proficiency is minimal and dated — roughly six months at NI (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis; the role requires 8–12 years of C++/STL as the primary language
No exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443-4-1), Secure Development Lifecycle practices, or industrial communication protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850)
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded-systems domain experience beyond a single early-career C project; the role is specifically for automation machinery manufacturing platforms
